description: Meta's Llama 3.2 3B — compact general-purpose assistant. Good balance of speed and capability for most laptops.
Meta's classic 8B instruct. Strong all-round explainer with broad world knowledge — a reliable choice for study help and learning new topics.
Meta's flagship 70B dense. Near-frontier general-purpose quality for workstation-class hardware. Last open-weights Llama as of 2026-04 (no Llama 5).

Meta's first natively multimodal MoE — 109B total / 17B active across 16 experts, 10M context. Vision is fused into the encoder (no separate mmproj). Shipped here at Q2_K_L (the only single-file quant Unsloth publishes); higher quants are split-folder and the current downloader can't follow them.

Alibaba's small dedicated coding model. Good for autocomplete and local coding assistance on modest hardware. No Qwen 3.5/3.6 Coder standalone exists yet — coding is folded into Qwen 3.6 27B / 35B-A3B.
Alibaba's 7B dedicated coding model. Community sweet spot for local code generation and refactoring at medium hardware tier.
Alibaba's general-purpose 7B. Strong instruction following and tool calling across many languages. Superseded by Qwen 3 8B for most workloads but kept for compatibility.
Alibaba's 32B dedicated coding dense model. Still a strong large-tier coder for workstation hardware; outperformed only by Qwen 3.6 27B / 35B-A3B which fold coding into general-purpose models.
Alibaba's mid-tier Qwen 3 dense reasoning model with 128K context and hybrid thinking. Bridges the gap between Qwen 3.5 9B and Qwen 3.6 27B.

Alibaba's compact 2B vision-language model with 256K context. Fits under 1.3 GB at Q4 with vision projector available. Catalog's lone minimal-tier entry and the universal fallback for low-RAM machines.
Alibaba's 4B dense vision-language model with thinking modes and 256K context. Fits 4 GB Macs with vision support.
Alibaba's edge-friendly 9B dense vision-language model with 256K context and hybrid thinking. Best general medium-tier pick from the Qwen 3.5 family.
Alibaba's flagship dense 27B with vision. Outperforms their own 397B MoE on agentic coding (SWE-bench Pro 53.5) and matches Claude 4.5 Opus on Terminal-Bench 2.0. Strong all-rounder.
Alibaba's sparse MoE vision-language model with only 3B active per token — runs at small-model speeds with frontier reasoning quality. SWE-bench Verified 73.4%, AIME 2026 92.7%.
architecture: MoE (35B total / 3B active, 256 experts, 8 routed + 1 shared)

description: Alibaba's 32B dense reasoning model trained with reinforcement learning. Matches DeepSeek R1 on math and code at ~5% of the parameters; the original `<think>`-style chain-of-thought specialist.
Mistral × NVIDIA collaboration. 12B with a 128K context window and strong multilingual coverage — works through long materials without losing the thread.
Mistral's 24B general-purpose model. Apache 2.0. Excellent at writing, multi-turn chat, and tool use.
Microsoft's 14B dense reasoning model. Strong at math, logic, and structured problem solving.
Google's Gemma 3 mid-size multimodal model. Vision + strong writing + tool calling. Kept as the only Gemma in the 12B class — Gemma 4 jumps from E4B (5B effective) straight to 26B-A4B and 31B.
Google's tiniest Gemma 4 — 2.3B effective with vision and audio input. Designed for on-device inference; only Q8_0 ships at this size, not Q4.
Google's edge-flagship 8B (4.5B effective) — vision + audio in, 140-language pretraining, Apache-2.0. Replaces Gemma 3 4B.
Google's Gemma 4 MoE — 3.8B active per token, 256K context, near-31B quality at 4B-class speed. Best speed/quality trade in the family.
Google's flagship dense Gemma 4 — top-3 open model on Arena, 256K context, Apache-2.0. Replaces Gemma 3 27B with stronger coding and reasoning.
DeepSeek's 16B MoE coding model (2.4B active per token). Uses Multi-head Latent Attention. Fast per-token throughput relative to size.
DeepSeek's smallest distilled reasoner on a 1.5B Qwen base. Surprisingly strong at competition math for its size; a tiny chain-of-thought specialist.
DeepSeek's 7B distilled reasoner. Surpasses GPT-4o on AIME at under 5 GB — best small-footprint pick for math and code chains.
DeepSeek's R1 distilled into Llama 3.1 8B. Cleaner English style than the Qwen-based distills, slightly behind on math.
DeepSeek's distilled reasoning model based on Qwen 14B. Lower hardware cost than the 32B version with most of the chain-of-thought quality.
DeepSeek's reasoning trace distilled into Qwen 2.5 32B. Long chain-of-thought, strong at math and code reasoning. MIT licensed.
DeepSeek's reasoning trace distilled into Llama 3.3 70B. Best open-weights large reasoning model that fits a 64+ GB MacBook. MIT licensed.

Xiaomi's first open-source reasoning LLM. A 7B trained from scratch for math and code that matches o1-mini and beats QwQ-32B-Preview on LiveCodeBench v6. MiMo's V2/V2.5 successors are all >300B and don't fit locally.
Xiaomi's vision-language MiMo. Pairs a native-resolution ViT with the MiMo-7B reasoner — strong on multimodal reasoning and OCR for its size.
OpenAI's permissively-licensed MoE model (~3.6B active per token). Apache 2.0. Natively released in MXFP4; bartowski re-quants cover the standard k-quant scheme.

OpenAI's policy-reasoning fork of gpt-oss-20B (released Oct 2025). Reads a policy at inference time and classifies content with chain-of-thought rationale — specialist, not a general chat replacement.

OpenAI's flagship open MoE — 117B total / 5.1B active per token, 128K context, near-parity with o4-mini reasoning. Apache 2.0. Shipped here as the F16 single file (65 GB) — the only single-file Unsloth quant; lower k-quants are all split-folder.
Moonshot's vision-language MoE with 16B total / 3B active params. Strong long-context multimodal reasoning with thinking mode.
Moonshot's hybrid linear-attention MoE — 48B total / 3B active, 1M native context with Kimi Delta Attention. 6× faster decoding on long inputs than dense peers.
architecture: MoE (48B total / 3B active, Kimi Delta Attention 3:1 KDA-to-MLA hybrid)

description: Z.ai's 9B reasoning-tuned chat model with deep-thinking mode and tool calling. Strong math and logic at medium-tier footprint.
Z.ai's 32B dense chat model with strong code generation and JSON tool calling, comparable to GPT-4o-class on IFEval and BFCL.
Z.ai's 30B-A3B MoE with Preserved Thinking — punches at 30B-class dense quality with ~3B active per token. Replaces GLM-4.5 Air. AIME 2025 91.6, SWE-bench Verified 59.2.
